channel.title: MCP News Feed
channel.description: Aggregated news about Model Context Protocol, AI agents, tool integrations, MCP security, and developer tooling.

item.title: I am building a wrapper tool for various API backend system to MCP server in a week.
item.description: But I am looking for a good tool to debugging the MCP and team collobration. Anyone can introduce one ?
item.dc:creator: Danny

item.title: NodeLLM 1.17: MCP Sampling, Concurrent Tool Execution, and Smarter ORM Control
item.description: Back when we introduced MCP support, we ended on a teaser: Phase 3 would tackle Sampling—letting servers request completions from the host instead of only exposing tools and resources to it. NodeLLM 1.17 delivers on that, and pairs it with a second, unrelated but overdue improvement: precise...
item.dc:creator: Shaiju Edakulangara

item.title: SEO Analyzer API — 5분 안에 프로젝트에 통합하기
item.description: SEO Analyzer API — 5분 안에 프로젝트에 통합하기 블로거, 마케터, SEO 에이전시, SaaS 랜딩페이지 프로젝트에 seo analyzer 기능이 필요한데 직접 구현하기엔 시간이 아까운가요? REST API 한 줄이면 됩니다. Quick Start # 즉시 테스트 (API 키 불필요) curl https://api.lazy-mac.com/seo/ 실전 예제 Python import requests resp = requests.get("https://api.lazy-mac.com/seo/", params={"q":...
item.dc:creator: 2x lazymac

item.title: MCP Tool: from Integration chaos to one clean connection
item.description: Every enterprise has a sprawling ecosystem of tools, APIs, and services. The dream has always been a single, clean way for AI agents to talk to all of them — without writing a custom integration for every endpoint. That dream now has a name: Model Context Protocol (MCP). In this post, we'll walk...
item.dc:creator: Halton Chen

item.title: Top 10 free MCP skills you can install right now (no signup, no payment)
item.description: Looking for free MCP skills? Here are 10 you can install right now — no payment, no signup, no credit card. 1. Arquitecto de Código Cero Alucinaciones Zero hallucinations code generation. If you don't know an exact library or API, it stops and asks. npx -y @marketnow/install...
item.dc:creator: Edison Flores

item.title: How to monetize your MCP server (x402, USDC, and the agent economy)
item.description: If you built an MCP server, you can sell it to AI agents globally. Here is how. The opportunity There are 8,560 MCP skills indexed on MarketNow. 675 developers install the MCP server every week. AI agents (Claude, Cursor, Cline) are actively looking for tools to install. But most MCP server...
item.dc:creator: Edison Flores

item.title: The Safari MCP Server Could Change How Developers Debug Websites
item.description: Apple shipped an official Safari MCP server in Safari Technology Preview 247, and it signals a real shift in how AI agents interact with browsers for debugging, testing, and automation. In early July 2026, Safari Technology Preview 247 shipped with something that caught the attention of every...
item.dc:creator: Parth Sharma

item.title: PaperQuire v0.3.0 — Your AI Agent's PDF Tool
item.description: AI agents can now generate PDFs Large language models are great at producing Markdown. What they can't do is turn that Markdown into a polished, branded PDF. That's always been a manual step — copy the output, paste it somewhere, fiddle with formatting, export. PaperQuire v0.3.0 removes that step...
item.dc:creator: paperquire

item.title: How to Monetize Your MCP Server in One Line of Code — x402 + Stripe
item.description: What is x402? x402 is the HTTP 402 Payment Required protocol — the payment rail built for AI agents. When an agent calls your MCP server and hasn't paid, your server responds with: { "x402Version": 2, "error": "payment_required", "accepts": [{ "scheme": "x402", "network": "eip155:8453", "amount":...
item.dc:creator: scotia1973-bot

item.title: MarketNow 2.0: 5 languages, 63 countries, 675 npm downloads — 2 weeks in
item.description: Two weeks ago we launched MarketNow — the trust layer for agent commerce. Here's what happened. By the numbers Metric Value Requests (Cloudflare) 55,807 Countries 63 npm weekly downloads 675 dev.to articles 8 MCP skills indexed 8,560 Free skills 43 Languages supported 5 (EN, ES, PT, ZH, FR)...
item.dc:creator: Edison Flores

item.title: We open-sourced our security audit. Here's what we found (and fixed).
item.description: We launched MarketNow 2 weeks ago. Then we got pentested. This is the honest report of what we found, what we fixed, and what's still pending. Why we did it MarketNow handles real money — USDC payments on Base for AI agent skills. Before we promote it broadly, we wanted to know: is it actually...
item.dc:creator: Edison Flores

item.title: We open-sourced our security audit
item.description: We launched MarketNow 2 weeks ago. Then we got pentested. This is the honest report of what we found, what we fixed, and what's still pending. Why we did it MarketNow handles real money — USDC payments on Base for AI agent skills. Before we promote it broadly, we wanted to know: is it actually...
item.dc:creator: Edison Flores

item.title: Apple just turned Safari into something AI agents can control
item.description: Earlier this week, Apple’s WebKit team shipped Safari Technology Preview 247 with a built-in Model Context Protocol server — 16 The post Apple just turned Safari into something AI agents can control appeared first on The New Stack.
item.dc:creator: Amanda Caswell

item.title: Agent Runs now available in the Vercel MCP and CLI
item.description: Your agent can now inspect Agent Runs via the Vercel MCP and CLI for eve, the open-source agent framework.eve traces are automatically ingested when deployed to Vercel and available as Agent Runs. The new Vercel MCP tools and Vercel CLI commands let you find projects with runs, list recent runs,...
item.dc:creator: Brooke Mosby

item.title: Announcing the Monetization Gateway: charge for any resource behind Cloudflare via x402
item.description: We're opening the waitlist for our Monetization Gateway, which will allow you to charge for any web page, dataset, API, or MCP tool behind Cloudflare. The charges will settle in stablecoins over the x402 open protocol, with no payments stack of your own to build.
item.dc:creator: Will Papper

item.title: Beta SDKs for the 2026-07-28 MCP Spec Release Candidate Are Here
item.description: Beta releases of the Python, TypeScript, Go, and C# SDKs are now available with support for the 2026-07-28 MCP specification release candidate. Here is what changes for your server, how to migrate, and how to test before the spec goes final on July 28.
item.dc:creator: Model Context Protocol

item.title: MCP for Beginners: Why Every AI Engineer and Developer Should Learn the Model Context Protocol
item.description: If you have spent any time building with large language models in the last year, you have hit the same wall everyone hits: your model is brilliant at reasoning but blind to the real world. It cannot read your database, call your internal API, search your documents, or trigger a deployment unless...
item.dc:creator: Lee_Stott

item.title: MCP Server Authorization with Azure API Management: From Simple to Advanced
item.description: Why put API Management in front of your MCP servers The Model Context Protocol (MCP) has quickly become the standard way for AI agents, such as GitHub Copilot in VS Code, to reach external tools and data. As soon as an MCP server does anything meaningful, the same questions that govern any API...
item.dc:creator: vzisiadis

item.title: Enterprise-Managed Authorization: Zero-touch OAuth for MCP
item.description: The Enterprise-Managed Authorization extension to the Model Context Protocol is now stable, enabling organizations to centrally provision MCP server access through their identity provider so users get connected servers on first login without per-app OAuth.
item.dc:creator: Model Context Protocol
